Our schools have now broken up for the Summer. What a challenging and extraordinary FR PAT WRITES:
year it has been for all concerned. Despite the removal of all legal restrictions, much uncertainty remains as
we come to the end of one academic year and look forward in joyful expectation to the new one. St. Hugh's
school has been magnificent throughout, and though neither Fr. Tomasz nor I have been able to visit for
more than a year, we have been regularly in touch virtually thanks to Church Services TV.
On Tuesday we were privileged to welcome the year 6 children in Church for the Leavers Mass. Fr. Tomasz
led the Mass; no more fitting way in which to celebrate their years at St. Hugh's and wish them well on the
next phase of life's journey. St. Hugh and St. John will continue to be their spiritual home.
On Wednesday it was my privilege to celebrate the End of Year Mass. Class 3P were present in church to
lead the readings and prayers, while the remainder of the school participated through 'live stream'. It was a
great privilege to be together as a parish to celebrate the Eucharist. The Mass is the goal and summit of our
The Sunday Mass obligation continues to be suspended and indications are this will continue to be so until
the first Sunday of Advent. The deeper obligation is that of love, and one of the positives of Covid-19 is to
deepen our appreciation of Mass as a personal encounter with the Lord rather than a virtual one. Over the
next six Sundays the Gospel readings will be from John Chapter 6, the Discourse on the Eucharist. As we
continue to live through these dark and uncertain times, we look to place our trust and hope in the Lord
